samples.text: If the soil is watered, then the seeds germinate. It is not the case that the soil is watered. Does it follow that the seeds germinate?
Whenever the soil is watered, the seeds germinate. However, in this situation it is not true that the soil is watered. Can we conclude that the seeds germinate?
If the soil is irrigated, then the seeds sprout. It is not the case that the soil is irrigated. Does it follow that the seeds sprout?
The only stated cause of 'the seeds germinate' is 'the soil is watered'. That cause did not occur. Can we conclude that the seeds germinate?
If the soil is watered, then the seeds germinate. It is not the case that the soil is watered. The meeting started at noon. Does it follow that the seeds germinate?
